title: alyssum lassiticum
intro: Alyssum lassiticum, also known as Madwort or Lessing's Alyssum, is a perennial plant in the mustard family that is native to Europe and Western Asia. Alyssum lassiticum has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it a potential treatment for wounds and skin conditions such as acne and eczema. The plant's medicinal properties also make it beneficial for reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues like bronchitis, and as a diuretic to alleviate urinary tract infections. Alyssum lassiticum can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be made by infusing the leaves and flowers in hot water or a solvent like ethanol.
